Before You Go
🧳
Arrive DFW by 9:00 AM
Allow 2+ hours before departure for check-in and security.
📱
Download offline: Google Maps, Google Translate (Japanese)
Also Hyperdia or Japan Transit Planner for train routing.
💴
Plan to get yen on arrival
7-Eleven and Japan Post ATMs at Haneda accept US cards. No need to exchange before leaving.

🏨 Grand Hyatt Tokyo
Address6-10-3 Roppongi, Minato-ku, Tokyo 106-0032
Main Phone+81-3-4333-1234

Afternoon
🏨
Check in to Grand Hyatt Tokyo, freshen up
Jet lag is real — keep the first evening light.
🚇
Get Suica / Pasmo IC Card
Rechargeable transit card for trains, buses, and convenience stores across Japan.
🏪
Convenience store run
7-Eleven, FamilyMart, Lawson — all excellent for snacks, drinks, and supplies. Japanese convenience store food genuinely surprises.
Evening
🍜
First ramen dinner
Ichiran (solo booths, no Japanese needed) is a perfect first-night pick — easy, delicious, low pressure.
💡 Aim for bed by 10 PM to reset your body clock fast.
📝 Notes
Day 2

🗼 Tokyo — Sakura Hoseki & Sumo

Thu, Jun 18 🌦 Tokyo forecast ▼
Morning · 9:00 AM
🏮
Senso-ji Temple & Nakamise Shopping Street
2-3-1 Asakusa, Taito-ku, Tokyo · Free entry · Open 24/7
Tokyo's oldest and most iconic temple. Walk through the Kaminarimon (Thunder Gate), browse the Nakamise shopping street for souvenirs, and explore the temple grounds.
💡 Arrive by 9:00 AM — crowds build quickly. The Nakamise vendors open around 10:00 AM so you'll have the temple relatively to yourselves first.
🚇 Depart Grand Hyatt by 8:20 AM
Step 1 — Walk 3 min to Roppongi Station (Tokyo Metro Hibiya Line · Exit 1C)
Step 2 — Take Hibiya Line toward Kita-Senju · 7 stops → Ningyocho Station (~14 min)
Step 3 — Transfer to Toei Asakusa Line toward Oshiage · 3 stops → Asakusa Station (~5 min)
Step 4 — Walk 5 min from Asakusa Station Exit A4 → Kaminarimon Gate
Total travel time: ~27–30 min · ¥280/person (use Suica card)
🚶
🕧 Depart Senso-ji by 12:30 PM → Sakura Hoseki
🚇 Metro (~12 min) or Walk (~18 min)
Metro: Walk 5 min → Asakusa Station (Ginza Line) · Take Ginza Line toward Shibuya (1 stop) → Ueno Station (~2 min) · Exit and walk ~8 min southwest to Kojima 1-9-2
Walk: Head southwest along Kokusai-dori (~18 min flat walk)
Taxi: ~5 min · ¥700–900 · Tell driver "Kojima Ichi-chome, Taito-ku"
Afternoon · 1:00 PM
💎
Sakura Hoseki — Jewelry Appointment
Taito-ku, Kojima 1-9-2, Tokyo · Search "Sakura Hoseki" on Google Maps
💡 From Senso-ji: 15–20 min walk southwest, or 5 min taxi.
🚶
🕝 Depart Sakura Hoseki by 2:30 PM → Asakusa Sumo Club
🚇 Metro (~8 min) or Walk (~18 min)
Metro: Walk to Ueno Station → Ginza Line toward Asakusa (1 stop) → Asakusa Station (~2 min) · Exit A4 · Walk 5 min north to 2-10-12 Asakusa
Walk: Head northeast back through Kappabashi-dori (~18 min)
Taxi: ~5 min · ¥700–900 · Tell driver "Asakusa Ni-chome, Taito-ku" (with kids — recommended)
Afternoon · 3:00 PM
🏆
Asakusa Sumo Club — Sumo Show & Chankonabe
1F, 2-10-12 Asakusa, Taito-ku, Tokyo 111-0032
💡 7–10 min walk from Senso-ji. 2-min walk from Tsukuba Express Asakusa Station. 6-min from Asakusa Station (Ginza/Asakusa lines).
🎟 Sumo Booking — Klook · Asakusa Sumo Club

🏨
🕗 Return to Grand Hyatt Tokyo after Sumo Show
🚇 Reverse of morning route — ~27–30 min · ¥280/person
Step 1 — Walk 2 min to Asakusa Station (Toei Asakusa Line)
Step 2 — Take Toei Asakusa Line toward Nishi-Magome · 3 stops → Ningyocho Station (~5 min)
Step 3 — Transfer to Tokyo Metro Hibiya Line toward Naka-Meguro · 7 stops → Roppongi Station (~14 min)
Step 4 — Walk 3 min from Exit 1C → Grand Hyatt Tokyo lobby
Use Suica cards — tap in, tap out.
📝 Notes
Day 3

🛍 Shibuya Day

Fri, Jun 19 🌦 Tokyo forecast ▼
Morning — Optional if out in time
⛩
Meiji Jingu Shrine — skip if running late
1-1 Yoyogikamizonocho, Shibuya-ku, Tokyo · Free entry · Open sunrise to sunset
Tokyo's most serene Shinto shrine — a forested sanctuary in the middle of the city. Walk the long forested path through tall cedar trees to the main hall. A peaceful contrast to the Shibuya energy ahead.
💡 Aim to leave Grand Hyatt by 9:30 AM to make this work. If you're out later than 10:30 AM, skip it and head straight to Shibuya Scramble — no stress.
🚇 Grand Hyatt → Meiji Jingu (~20 min)
Walk 3 min → Roppongi Station · Hibiya Line toward Naka-Meguro (2 stops) → Ebisu · JR Yamanote Line (2 stops) → Harajuku · Walk 2 min to shrine entrance · ¥200/person
Meiji Jingu → Takeshita Street: Walk straight out the south exit of the shrine → Takeshita Street is right there (~3 min)
~11:30 AM · Shibuya Scramble
☕
If skipping Meiji Jingu — depart Grand Hyatt ~11:00 AM
🚇 Grand Hyatt → Shibuya (~15 min)
Walk 3 min → Roppongi Station · Hibiya Line toward Naka-Meguro (2 stops) → Ebisu · JR Yamanote Line (1 stop) → Shibuya · ¥200/person
~11:15 AM · Shibuya Scramble
🚶
Shibuya Scramble Crossing
Outside Shibuya Station, Shibuya-ku
Experience the crossing at street level first — then head straight into Scramble Square for your 11:40 Shibuya Sky slot.
11:40 AM · Shibuya Sky — Rooftop Observatory
🌆
Shibuya Sky — Scramble Square Rooftop
Shibuya Scramble Square · 45F–46F · 2-24-12 Shibuya, Shibuya-ku
Open-air rooftop observatory at 230m — looking straight down at the Scramble Crossing below. On a clear day Mt. Fuji is visible to the west. Sky swing for the kids.
💡 Time slot: 11:40 AM · Allow ~60 min · Present mobile voucher QR at entry · No re-entry after exit.
🎟 Shibuya Sky Booking

~1:00 PM · Shop down through Scramble Square
🖤
Saint Laurent — Scramble Square · 3F
2-24-12 Shibuya, Shibuya-ku · Scramble Square 3F
Full YSL collection — ready-to-wear, handbags, shoes, and accessories. Open until 9:00 PM.
💡 Enter Scramble Square from street level and go to 3F first.
✨
ReFa — Scramble Square · 6F (+Q BEAUTY)
2-24-12 Shibuya, Shibuya-ku · Scramble Square 6F · +81-3-6452-6207
Premium beauty devices — facial rollers, hair tools, and Japan-exclusive models at better-than-US pricing.
💡 Head up from 3F to 6F — tax-free with passport. Then exit Scramble Square and head northeast toward Jinnan.
~12:30 PM · 5 min walk northeast
👟
Onitsuka Tiger — Shibuya · Jinnan
1-21-3 Jinnan, Shibuya-ku, Tokyo 150-0041 · 1F & 2F
Japan's iconic heritage sneaker brand — exclusive colorways and Japan-only releases. Open until 8:00 PM.
💡 On the way from Scramble Square to Harajuku — walk northeast along Jinnan-dori. 5 min from Scramble Square.
~1:00 PM · Continue northeast into Harajuku
🛍
Takeshita Street — Harajuku
Takeshita-dori, Jingumae, Shibuya-ku
Japan's most famous youth fashion street — crepes, character shops, wild fashion, and great energy. The kids will love every inch of it.
💡 3-min walk from Onitsuka Tiger. Grab a crepe and browse at your own pace — allow 30–45 min.
🥤
Iyoshi Cola — Jingumae
5-29-12 Jingumae, Shibuya-ku, Tokyo
Tokyo's finest craft cola — handmade with spices, citrus, and botanicals. A must for the whole family.
💡 5-min walk from the end of Takeshita Street. Opens 1:00 PM · Closes 7:00 PM. Time it right — go early before they sell out.
~2:30 PM · Walk back to Shibuya (~12 min)
🛒
MEGA Don Quijote — Shibuya
28-6 Udagawacho, Shibuya-ku, Tokyo 150-0042 · +81 570-076-311
Japan's ultimate discount store — snacks, cosmetics, electronics, souvenirs, character goods and everything in between. Great for gifts to bring home.
💡 Open 24 hours · Tax-free counter available · 5-min walk from Shibuya Scramble back toward the station.
Evening
🍽
Dinner in Shibuya
Plenty of great options in the area — ramen, izakaya, tonkatsu, or rooftop dining. Add your reservation here when booked.
🏨
Return to Grand Hyatt Tokyo
🚇 Shibuya → Grand Hyatt (~15 min)
JR Yamanote Line (1 stop) → Ebisu · Transfer to Hibiya Line toward Kita-Senju (2 stops) → Roppongi · Walk 3 min to Grand Hyatt · ¥200/person
Tonight — Before Bed
🧳
Pack for Kyoto & Osaka leg — depart tomorrow
You head to Kyoto (half day) then Osaka tomorrow morning. Pack a travel bag for Jun 20–22 tonight so the morning is smooth.
💡 Luggage storage at Grand Hyatt: Leave any bags you don't need for the Osaka leg at the hotel's luggage storage (concierge desk). They'll hold them securely until you return on Jun 22. Call ahead to confirm: +81-3-4333-8800
Consider using Takkyubin (luggage forwarding) to ship bags directly to your Osaka hotel overnight — ask the concierge to arrange. Costs ~¥2,000–2,500/bag and arrives the next day.
📝 Notes
Day 4

⛩ Tokyo → Kyoto (½ day) → 🐙 Osaka

Sat, Jun 20 🌦 Kyoto forecast 🌦 Osaka forecast ▼
Morning — Tokyo Departure
🚄
Shinkansen: Shinagawa → Kyoto · NOZOMI 21 · Green Car
Depart Shinagawa Station · Arrive Kyoto Station · 2h 07m
💡 Sit on the right side (seats B/C/D face right when heading west) for Mt. Fuji views between Shin-Fuji and Mishima stations — best around 10:10–10:20 AM.
🚄 Shinkansen Booking — Smart EX · Green Car

⚠ Depart from Shinagawa Station — not Tokyo Station.

🚕 By Taxi (recommended · ~15–20 min on a Saturday morning)
Grand Hyatt Tokyo → Shinagawa Station is roughly 5 km via Roppongi-dori and the Dai-ichi Keihin expressway. Fare: approx. ¥2,000–2,800.

Timing for 9:37 AM departure:
  • 8:50 AM — Taxi departs Grand Hyatt lobby
  • 9:05–9:15 AM — Arrive Shinagawa Station (Shinkansen entrance, south side)
  • 9:15 AM — Head to platform (scan QR tickets at Shinkansen gate, follow signs to Nozomi/Hikari platforms)
  • 9:25 AM — Board Car 10 · Shinkansen doors open ~10 min before departure
  • 9:37 AM — Departs 🚄

💡 Ask the hotel concierge to call a taxi the night before or 30 min early — Grand Hyatt can arrange this. Tell the driver: "Shinagawa Station, Shinkansen entrance (新幹線口)"
Morning/Afternoon — Kyoto Stopover (~4–5 hours)
⛩
Fushimi Inari Shrine
Thousands of orange torii gates winding up a mountain. One of Japan's most iconic sights. Free, open 24/7, a 10-min train from Kyoto Station.
💡 From Kyoto Station: JR Nara Line 2 stops to Inari Station (5 min). Walk the lower gates — you don't need the full hike to be amazed.
🍱
Lunch near Fushimi or Nishiki Market
Nishiki Market ("Kyoto's Kitchen") is a narrow covered market packed with pickles, grilled skewers, tofu, and Kyoto specialties.
🎋
Gion District walk (if time allows)
Kyoto's famous geisha district — lantern-lit stone lanes, wooden machiya townhouses. A 15-min bus from Nishiki.
Afternoon — Kyoto → Osaka (settle in)
🚄
Kyoto → Osaka · Shinkansen
Depart Kyoto Station · ~15 min to Shin-Osaka. Depart by 2–3 PM to arrive and check in with daylight to spare.
🚄 Shinkansen Tickets — Kyoto → Osaka · NOZOMI 439

TrainNOZOMI 439
Departs5:33 PM · Kyoto
Arrives5:48 PM · Shin-Osaka

🏨
Check in — Caption by Hyatt Osaka
Dotonbori / Namba area is ideal. Everything is walkable.
Evening — Welcome to Osaka
🦞
Dotonbori — Osaka's neon food district
The famous Glico running man sign, giant crab restaurant signs, street food everywhere. Loud, electric, and delicious.
💡 Must eat tonight: takoyaki (octopus balls) and okonomiyaki. Walk and eat along the canal.
📝 Notes
Day 5

🐙 Osaka — Full Day

Sun, Jun 21 🌦 Osaka forecast ▼
🏨 Caption by Hyatt Osaka Shinsaibashi — Quick Reference
Address1-9-20 Shinsaibashisuji, Chuo-ku, Osaka
Phone+81-6-4400-5100
NearestShinsaibashi · 5 min to Dotonbori
Morning
🏯
Osaka Castle
Magnificent 16th-century castle surrounded by moats and gardens. The interior museum covers Osaka's fascinating feudal history. Beautiful in morning light.
🌳
Osaka Castle Park walk
One of Osaka's largest parks — good for a morning stroll before the day heats up.
Afternoon
🐟
Kuromon Ichiba Market
"Osaka's Kitchen" — 170+ stalls with fresh seafood, produce, and street food. Much more local feel than tourist markets. Vendors grill fresh seafood right in front of you.
💡 Come hungry. This is one of the best food experiences in all of Japan.
🛍
Shinsaibashi Shopping Arcade
One of Japan's oldest covered shopping streets. Mix of luxury brands, Japanese labels, and street fashion.
🎡
Osaka Aquarium Kaiyukan (optional)
One of the world's largest aquariums — whale sharks, manta rays, and a stunning 8-story central tank.
Evening
🍖
Yakiniku (Japanese BBQ) dinner
Osaka is one of the best cities for yakiniku. Grill your own wagyu beef at the table.
🌃
Namba & Amerika-Mura evening
Osaka's youth fashion district — street art, vintage shops, record stores. Very different energy from Tokyo.
📝 Notes
Day 6

🐙 Osaka Morning → 🗼 Back to Tokyo

Mon, Jun 22 🌦 Osaka forecast ▼
🏨 Caption by Hyatt Osaka Shinsaibashi — Quick Reference
Address1-9-20 Shinsaibashisuji, Chuo-ku, Osaka
Phone+81-6-4400-5100
NearestShinsaibashi · 5 min to Dotonbori
Morning — Final Osaka
🧧
Final Osaka shopping & souvenirs
Osaka-specific gifts: takoyaki kits, Glico candy, local sake, Pocky varieties you can't get in the US.
💡 Use the tax-free counter at major department stores (passport required) — save 10% on eligible purchases over ¥5,000.
🏯
Umeda Sky Building (morning visit)
Osaka's most dramatic observatory with a floating garden rooftop. Less crowded in the morning.
🍱
Lunch before departure
One last Osaka meal — kushikatsu (fried skewers) is the Osaka specialty you shouldn't miss.
Late Afternoon — Osaka → Tokyo
🚄
Shinkansen: Shin-Osaka → Tokyo (~2h 30m)
Depart Osaka in the late afternoon. You'll arrive in Tokyo by early evening with time to check in and decompress.
💡 Book a reserved seat. The Nozomi is fastest (Tokyo Station). Bring food from the station — Shinkansen bento boxes are excellent.
🏨
Check in — Grand Hyatt Tokyo
Back at the Grand Hyatt for your final stretch in Tokyo.
🏨 Grand Hyatt Tokyo — Quick Reference
Address6-10-3 Roppongi, Minato-ku, Tokyo 106-0032
Main Phone+81-3-4333-1234
Concierge+81-3-4333-8800
Nearest StationRoppongi (Hibiya & Oedo Lines) · 3 min walk
Evening
🍜
Low-key dinner near the hotel
Travel day — keep it simple and nearby. Any ramen or soba shop will do.
📝 Notes
Day 7

🗼 Tokyo — Akihabara & Ginza

Tue, Jun 23 🌦 Tokyo forecast ▼
Morning
⚡
Akihabara Electric Town
Electronics, anime, gaming, and pop culture hub. Even without anime interest, the scale and energy are worth experiencing.
🐟
Tsukiji Outer Market
The outer market (still operating) has incredible fresh sushi, tamagoyaki, and street food. Best for breakfast/brunch.
💡 Go before 10 AM — vendors sell out of the best items early.
Afternoon
💎
Ginza district
Tokyo's most upscale shopping and dining district. Apple Store, flagship luxury brands, beautiful architecture.
🏛
teamLab Planets or Borderless (if not done)
This is a good backup day for digital art if you didn't go during the first Tokyo leg.
Evening
🍣
Omakase sushi dinner (book in advance)
For a special night — chef's choice sushi is a transformative experience in Tokyo.
📝 Notes
Day 8

🗼 Tokyo — Ueno & Yanaka

Wed, Jun 24 🌦 Tokyo forecast ▼
Morning
🏛
Ueno Park & Museums
Ueno is home to multiple world-class museums: Tokyo National Museum (Japan's largest), National Museum of Nature and Science, and the Western Art Museum.
🦁
Ueno Zoo (optional)
Home to Japan's famous giant pandas. Fun if you want a relaxed morning.
Afternoon
🏘
Yanaka neighborhood walk
One of the few Tokyo neighborhoods that survived WWII — old-town atmosphere, temples, artisan shops, and the famous Yanaka Ginza shopping street.
💡 This is the most "old Tokyo" you can experience. Very different from Shibuya or Shinjuku.
🍵
Afternoon tea / matcha café
Several excellent traditional tea rooms in the Ueno/Yanaka area.
Evening
🌃
Roppongi dinner & rooftop bar
Upscale dining, international restaurants, and rooftop bars. Roppongi Hills has great options with city views.
📝 Notes
Day 9

🗼 Tokyo — Day Trip or Deep Dive

Thu, Jun 25 🌦 Tokyo forecast ▼
Option A — Nikko Day Trip
🏔
Nikko (2h from Tokyo by train)
UNESCO-listed temples and shrines in a dramatic mountain setting. Very different from the city — waterfalls, cedar forests, ornate mausoleums.
💡 Take the Tobu Nikko Express from Asakusa Station. Full-day trip with an early departure.
Option B — Kamakura Day Trip
🧘
Kamakura (1h from Tokyo)
Seaside town with the famous 13-meter Great Buddha (Daibutsu), multiple Zen temples, and hiking trails between shrines.
💡 JR Yokosuka Line from Tokyo Station. Combine with Enoshima Island for a full day.
Option C — Stay in Tokyo
♨
Onsen day spa
LaQua (near Tokyo Dome) or Oedo Onsen Monogatari (Odaiba) — natural hot spring baths in the city. A true Japanese relaxation experience.
🎮
Tokyo Skytree
634m — the world's tallest broadcast tower. Observatory deck with clear day views to Mt. Fuji.
📝 Notes
Day 10

🗼 Tokyo — Final Day

Fri, Jun 26 🌦 Tokyo forecast ▼
Morning
🛍
Final shopping — anything missed
Don Quijote (discount variety store, open 24h), Tokyu Hands, Loft — great for last-minute gifts and Japanese products you can't find at home.
📦
Pack and organize bags
If you used luggage forwarding, arrange airport delivery. Weigh bags — know your airline's limits before getting to the airport.
Afternoon
🍱
Depachika (department store basement food hall)
Takashimaya, Isetan, or Mitsukoshi — the basement food halls are a final Japan food experience not to miss. Pick up flight snacks too.
🌆
Last Tokyo views — wherever feels right
Wander your favorite neighborhood one more time. Tokyo rewards slow walking.
Evening — Last Dinner in Japan
🍱
Special farewell dinner (book in advance)
Make it count — a proper Japanese restaurant experience for your last night. Teppanyaki, kaiseki, or high-end ramen, whatever feels most meaningful.
🌃
Night walk / final drinks
A sake bar or rooftop drinks to close out the trip properly.
📝 Notes
Day 11

✈ Return — Tokyo Haneda to DFW

Sat, Jun 27 🌦 Tokyo forecast ▼
✈ HND → DFW · AA 176 · Economy
Departs4:30 PM · HND Terminal 3
Arrives2:15 PM · DFW (same day)
Duration11h 45m nonstop
Seats26L & 26K
MealDinner + Snack
AircraftBoeing 777-300ER
Morning Plan
☕
Relaxed morning — hotel check-out
No rush this morning. Enjoy a final convenience store breakfast or nearby café.
🚇
Hotel → Haneda (HND)
Tokyo Monorail from Hamamatsucho, or Keikyu Line from Shinagawa. Both take ~30 min. Allow plenty of time.
💡 International departures: HND Terminal 3. Arrive by 1:30 PM — 3 hours before your 4:30 PM flight. AA check-in closes 60 min before departure.
🏪
Final convenience store run at the airport
7-Eleven inside Haneda has excellent onigiri, sandwiches, and snacks to bring on the plane.
📝 Notes
💡 Essential Japan Tips

💴 Money

  • Japan is largely cash-based — carry yen at all times
  • 7-Eleven and Japan Post ATMs accept US cards reliably
  • IC card (Suica/Pasmo) works for trains and convenience stores
  • Tipping is NOT done — it can be considered rude

🚇 Getting Around

  • JR Pass covers Shinkansen bullet trains (buy before departure)
  • Google Maps works excellently for train routing in Japan
  • Taxis are expensive — trains for almost everything
  • Luggage forwarding (takkyubin) ships bags between hotels overnight

📱 Tech

  • Rent pocket WiFi or buy data SIM at Haneda airport
  • Download Google Translate Japanese pack (offline)
  • Hyperdia or Google Maps for train schedules
  • TableAll / Tablecheck / OpenTable for restaurant reservations

🎌 Etiquette

  • Remove shoes entering homes and many traditional restaurants
  • Eating/drinking while walking is generally frowned upon
  • Speak softly on trains and public transit
  • Cash and cards go in the tray at registers — not hand-to-hand

🌧 June Weather (Rainy Season)

  • June is tsuyu (rainy season) — pack a compact umbrella
  • Temperatures: 22–30°C (72–86°F) — warm and humid
  • Light, breathable clothing recommended
  • Rain rarely lasts all day — morning plans usually still work

🍱 Food Must-Haves

  • Tokyo: Ramen, sushi, tonkatsu, yakitori, depachika
  • Kyoto: Matcha sweets, tofu dishes, Nishiki Market bites
  • Osaka: Takoyaki, okonomiyaki, kushikatsu, yakiniku
  • Everywhere: Convenience store food is genuinely world-class
